title: Apply: The Legislative Branch
description: Apply your knowledge about the text and history of the legislative branch, including the founders' vision and Congress today.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which scenario is an example of the legislative branch performing one of its enumerated powers?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress passes a bill that makes it a crime for people to purchase and own firearm silencers.
Congress approves a declaration of war against several countries.
Congress passes a bill that requires all states hold elections for governors on the first of January.
Congress approves a resolution that dictates that Christianity is the national religion of the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which scenario is an example of the legislative branch performing one of its enumerated powers?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress passes a bill that allows people to buy titles of nobility.
Congress creates a law that states all people must house a member of the military for three years.
Congress imposes a ban on the use of jury trials for civil cases.
Congress authorizes the construction of a high-speed interstate rail network.

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from the majority opinion in *United States v. Lopez* (1995).**

The possession of a gun in a local school zone is in no sense an economic activity that might, through repetition elsewhere, substantially affect any sort of interstate commerce. [[☃ definition 1]] was a local student at a local school; there is no indication that he had recently moved in interstate commerce, and there is no requirement that his possession of the firearm have any concrete tie to interstate commerce.

To uphold the Government's [[☃ definition 2]] here, we would have to pile inference upon inference in a manner that would [[☃ definition 3]] to convert congressional authority under the Commerce Clause to a general police power of the sort retained by the States.

**Which statement best summarizes the argument in this excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: the defendant in some legal proceedings; the person who must respond to a legal petition

choices.content: The power to regulate interstate commerce cannot be used to regulate the possession of firearms in school zones,  as such regulations are unrelated to interstate commerce.
The possession of weapons in a school zone poses a clear and present danger, making regulations of firearms  in school zones constitutional.
The possession of guns in a local school zone substantially affects interstate commerce, so Congress has general police power to regulate firearms.
The possession of weapons in publicly funded buildings like schools is something Congress cannot regulate, as this power is granted to the states under the Second Amendment.

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from the majority opinion in *McCulloch v. Maryland* (1819) written by Chief Justice John Marshall.**

[T]he sound construction of the constitution must allow to the national legislature that [[☃ definition 1]], with respect to the means by which the powers it [[☃ definition 2]] are to be carried into [[☃ definition 3]], which will enable that body to perform the high duties assigned to it, in the manner most beneficial to the people. Let the [[☃ definition 4]] be legitimate, let it be within the scope of the constitution, and all [[☃ definition 5]] which are appropriate, which are plainly adapted to that end, which are not prohibited, but consist with the letter and spirit of the constitution, are constitutional. . . .

**Which statement best summarizes the argument in this excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: the act of doing something or carrying out a plan

choices.content: The Constitution grants Congress the flexibility to take actions to carry out their listed powers, as long those actions are consistent with the Constitution’s text and principles.
The Constitution clearly lists the powers that Congress does and does not have, and any laws beyond the scope of Congress’s listed powers are unconstitutional and need to be struck down.
The Constitution gives Congress a wide range of powers that cannot be limited by states or local governments, as a strong national government is essential to a well-functioning democracy.
The Constitution was written in a way to balance the powers between the federal and state governments, and any powers not specifically given to Congress should be reserved to the states.

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from “Letter to the Republican,” written in 1787 under the [[☃ definition 2]] Federal Farmer.**

Independent of the opinions of many great authors, that a free elective government cannot be extended over large territories, a few reflections must [[☃ definition 1]], that one government and general legislation alone never can extend equal benefits to all parts of the United States. Different laws, customs, and opinions exist in the different states, which by a uniform system of laws would be unreasonably invaded.

**Which statement best summarizes the concern that Federal Farmer has about the structure of the U.S. government, as described in the U.S. Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress does not have a clear legislative process, making it difficult for the federal government to implement necessary laws.
Congress is not elected by the people, which means it does not have to represent their interests when making new laws.
Congress could not accurately reflect the different viewpoints of all citizens, as it was too far removed from the people.
Congress has too many representatives in both chambers, leading to partisan debates and gridlock.

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from “Letter to the Republican,” written in 1787 under the [[☃ definition 2]] Federal Farmer.**

Independent of the opinions of many great authors, that a free elective government cannot be extended over large territories, a few reflections must [[☃ definition 1]], that one government and general legislation alone never can extend equal benefits to all parts of the United States. Different laws, customs, and opinions exist in the different states, which by a uniform system of laws would be unreasonably invaded.

**The views expressed in this passage most align with the views of which group?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Federalists
The Anti-Federalists
The Loyalists
The Patriots

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from an article printed in the *Maryland Gazette* in 1788 and written by Luther Martin, an Anti-Federalist.**

Seventy years is estimated for the life of a man; it can hardly be supposed, that a senator, especially from the States remote from the seat of empire, will accept of an appointment which must [[☃ definition 1]] him for six years from his State, without giving up to a great degree his [[☃ definition 2]] in his own State. If he has a family, he will take his family with him to the place where the government shall be fixed, that will become his home, and there is every reason to expect that his future views and prospects will center in the favors and [[☃ definition 3]] either of the general government, or of the government of that State where the seat of empire is established:—In either case, he is lost to his own State. 

**Which statement best summarizes the concern Martin expresses in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: benefits that come from employment or holding political office

choices.content: The term limits of senators are too restrictive and will not allow for any person to make meaningful decisions in their role as senator.
State legislatures are responsible for appointing new senators, giving the states too much power over the federal government.
Senators hold significant power over the nomination and appointment processes, making the federal government more like a monarchy than a republic.
Senators’ lengthy terms will make them more accountable to the national government than to the constituents of their own states.

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is an excerpt from an article printed in the *Maryland Gazette* in 1788 and written by Luther Martin, an Anti-Federalist.**

Seventy years is estimated for the life of a man; it can hardly be supposed, that a senator, especially from the States remote from the seat of empire, will accept of an appointment which must [[☃ definition 1]] him for six years from his State, without giving up to a great degree his [[☃ definition 2]] in his own State. If he has a family, he will take his family with him to the place where the government shall be fixed, that will become his home, and there is every reason to expect that his future views and prospects will center in the favors and [[☃ definition 3]] either of the general government, or of the government of that State where the seat of empire is established:—In either case, he is lost to his own State. 

**This excerpt best serves as evidence to support which statement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: benefits that come from employment or holding political office

choices.content: Some citizens feared that the legislative branch had too much power.
Some framers wanted the people to directly elect their senators.
Some Anti-Federalists refused to ratify the Constitution without a Bill of Rights.
Some Loyalists compromised on representation in Congress to ensure the Constitution would be ratified.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which statement best describes the message of the political cartoon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: Taylor, C.J. “The Way We Become Senator Nowadays,” *Puck Magazine,* [United States Senate Website](https://www.senate.gov/art-artifacts/historical-images/political-cartoons-caricatures/38_00598.htm), 1890.

options.alt: A man in a suit and a top hat holds a one million dollar bag of money over a hole in a machine. The machine has a sign that reads “Put a million dollars in the slot and get a senatorship.” The bottom of the machine reads, “State Legislature.” A mechanical arm extends from the machine to a door labeled “U.S. Senate.”
options.caption: “The Way We Become Senator Nowadays” by C.J. Taylor, 1890

choices.content: The selection process for the Senate is flawed and allows corrupt people to serve as senators.
Candidates for Senate spend all of their time raising money for their elections, rather than performing their legislative duties.
State legislatures have become so partisan and divided that they cannot agree on candidates for the Senate.
The Senate has become too aristocratic, as the Constitution allows only wealthy people to serve in office.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which amendment to the U.S. Constitution addressed the issue shown in this cartoon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: Taylor, C.J. “The Way We Become Senator Nowadays,” *Puck Magazine,* [United States Senate Website](https://www.senate.gov/art-artifacts/historical-images/political-cartoons-caricatures/38_00598.htm), 1890.

options.alt: A man in a suit and a top hat holds a one million dollar bag of money over a hole in a machine. The machine has a sign that reads “Put a million dollars in the slot and get a senatorship.” The bottom of the machine reads, “State Legislature.” A mechanical arm extends from the machine to a door labeled “U.S. Senate.”
options.caption: “The Way We Become Senator Nowadays” by C.J. Taylor, 1890

choices.content: The Sixteenth Amendment
The Seventeenth Amendment
The Eighteenth Amendment
The Nineteenth Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which statement best describes the message of the political cartoon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A man with a hat that reads “Congress” is stumbling down a set of stairs leading out of a building. A rope labeled “Filibuster” is looped around his neck and pulling him back into the building. His hat is coming off his head and he has dropped items he’s carrying, including a bag labeled “Hurry-Calls from Home” and a toolbox labeled “The Fence-Builders Friend.”
options.caption: “Not Yet But Soon” by Clifford Berryman, 1912

choices.content: Representatives in Congress always vote in line with their constituents.
The different chambers of Congress must work together to pass laws.
Representatives in Congress can never return to their home states.
The filibuster slows down the legislative process.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which scenario is an example of Congress performing a power that is specifically denied in Article I, Section 9?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress institutes a new sales tax on soft drinks to encourage people to adopt a healthier lifestyle.
Congress creates a new court system between the U.S. courts of appeals and the Supreme Court.
Congress passes a bill that gives members of Congress who have served for at least 25 years the title of “Lord” or “Lady.”
Congress passes a law that adds four justices to the Supreme Court, increasing the number of justices to 13.
